# (local) Burst Probabilities?


A natural way to probe local higher-order structure is to study the number of
neighbours lying very close to a typical observed point.

For a point $x_i$ in the pattern, define its local neighbour count at radius $r$ by

$$
C_i(r) = \#\{x_j \in X \setminus \{x_i\} : \|x_j - x_i\| \le r\}.
$$

This counts how many further points lie within distance $r$ of $x_i$, and so
measures the local “burstiness” of the pattern around an observed point.

We are particularly interested in tail probabilities of the form

$$
P(C_i(r) \ge m),
$$

which describe the probability that a typical point belongs to a dense local
cluster of size at least $m+1$.

This is a useful intermediate statistic. The mean of $C_i(r)$ is closely related
to second-order structure, but the full distribution --- and especially its
upper tail --- reflects the frequency of small, dense local bursts involving
multiple nearby points. In this sense, burst probabilities may capture local
higher-order behaviour without requiring direct estimation of third-order
product densities.

```{R}
library(spatstat)
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# ============================================================
# Settings
# ============================================================

set.seed(1)

W <- spatstat.geom::owin(c(0, 1), c(0, 1))

lambda <- 100
phi    <- 1.5
s_cscp <- 0.05
s_lgcp <- 0.68 * s_cscp   # use your matched LGCP scale

n_sims <- 200

r_vals <- c(0.02, 0.04, 0.06, 0.08)
m_vals <- 1:6

# ============================================================
# Helper: neighbour counts C_i(r)
# ============================================================

point_burst_counts <- function(X, r) {
  n <- spatstat.geom::npoints(X)

  if (n == 0) return(integer(0))
  if (n == 1) return(0L)

  dmat <- spatstat.geom::pairdist(X)
  counts <- rowSums(dmat <= r) - 1L   # subtract self-count
  as.integer(counts)
}

# ============================================================
# Estimate burst probabilities for one pattern
# ============================================================

burst_profile_one <- function(X, r_vals, m_vals) {
  purrr::map_dfr(r_vals, function(r) {
    Ci <- point_burst_counts(X, r)

    tibble(
      r = r,
      m = m_vals,
      prob = sapply(m_vals, function(m) mean(Ci >= m))
    )
  })
}

# ============================================================
# Simulate one pattern from each model
# ============================================================

simulate_pattern <- function(model) {
  if (model == "LGCP") {
    sim_lgcp(W, lambda = lambda, phi = phi, s = s_lgcp)$pp
  } else {
    sim_cscp(W, lambda = lambda, phi = phi, s = s_cscp, df = 1)$pp
  }
}

# ============================================================
# Run pilot study
# ============================================================

pilot_df <- purrr::map_dfr(c("LGCP", "CSCP"), function(model) {
  purrr::map_dfr(seq_len(n_sims), function(rep) {
    X <- simulate_pattern(model)

    burst_profile_one(X, r_vals, m_vals) %>%
      mutate(model = model, rep = rep)
  })
})

# Average over simulations
pilot_summary <- pilot_df %>%
  group_by(model, r, m) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_prob = mean(prob),
    lo = quantile(prob, 0.1),
    hi = quantile(prob, 0.9),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ============================================================
# Plot
# ============================================================

ggplot(pilot_summary, aes(x = m, y = mean_prob, colour = model, group = model)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 1.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ r, labeller = label_bquote(r == .(r))) +
  labs(
    x = "Threshold m",
    y = expression(hat(P)(C[i](r) >= m)),
    title = "Pilot burst probabilities for matched LGCP and CSCP models"
  ) +
  theme_bw()
```
